verses.latin_vulgate: Domine, Deus meus es tu ;  exaltabo te, et confitebor nomini tuo :  quoniam fecisti mirabilia,  cogitationes antiquas fideles. Amen.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: יְהֹוָ֤ה אֱלֹהַי֙ אַתָּ֔ה אֲרֽוֹמִמְךָ֙ אוֹדֶ֣ה שִׁמְךָ֔ כִּ֥י עָשִׂ֖יתָ פֶּ֑לֶא עֵצ֥וֹת מֵרָחֹ֖ק אֱמ֥וּנָה אֹֽמֶן׃
verses.king_james: O Lord, thou art my God; I will exalt thee, I will praise thy name; for thou hast done wonderful things; thy counsels of old are faithfulness and truth.

verses.latin_vulgate: Quia posuisti civitatem in tumulum,  urbem fortem in ruinam, domum alienorum :  ut non sit civitas,  et in sempiternum non ædificetur.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כִּ֣י שַׂ֤מְתָּ מֵעִיר֙ לַגָּ֔ל קִרְיָ֥ה בְצוּרָ֖ה לְמַפֵּלָ֑ה אַרְמ֤וֹן זָרִים֙ מֵעִ֔יר לְעוֹלָ֖ם לֹ֥א יִבָּנֶֽה׃
verses.king_james: For thou hast made of a city an heap; of a defenced city a ruin: a palace of strangers to be no city; it shall never be built.

verses.latin_vulgate: Super hoc laudabit te populus fortis ;  civitas gentium robustarum timebit te :
verses.hebrew_masoretic: עַל־כֵּ֖ן יְכַבְּד֣וּךָ עַם־עָ֑ז קִרְיַ֛ת גּוֹיִ֥ם עָרִיצִ֖ים יִירָאֽוּךָ׃
verses.king_james: Therefore shall the strong people glorify thee, the city of the terrible nations shall fear thee.

verses.latin_vulgate: quia factus es fortitudo pauperi,  fortitudo egeno in tribulatione sua,  spes a turbine,  umbraculum ab æstu ;  spiritus enim robustorum  quasi turbo impellens parietem.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כִּי־הָיִ֨יתָ מָע֥וֹז לַדָּ֛ל מָע֥וֹז לָאֶבְי֖וֹן בַּצַּר־ל֑וֹ מַחְסֶ֤ה מִזֶּ֙רֶם֙ צֵ֣ל מֵחֹ֔רֶב כִּ֛י ר֥וּחַ עָרִיצִ֖ים כְּזֶ֥רֶם קִֽיר׃
verses.king_james: For thou hast been a strength to the poor, a strength to the needy in his distress, a refuge from the storm, a shadow from the heat, when the blast of the terrible ones is as a storm against the wall.

verses.latin_vulgate: Sicut æstus in siti,  tumultum alienorum humiliabis ;  et quasi calore sub nube torrente,  propaginem fortium marcescere facies.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כְּחֹ֣רֶב בְּצָי֔וֹן שְׁא֥וֹן זָרִ֖ים תַּכְנִ֑יעַ חֹ֚רֶב בְּצֵ֣ל עָ֔ב זְמִ֥יר עָרִיצִ֖ים יַעֲנֶֽה׃ {פ}
verses.king_james: Thou shalt bring down the noise of strangers, as the heat in a dry place; even the heat with the shadow of a cloud: the branch of the terrible ones shall be brought low.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et faciet Dominus exercituum  omnibus populis in monte hoc  convivium pinguium,  convivium vindemiæ,  pinguium medullatorum,  vindemiæ defæcatæ.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְעָשָׂה֩ יְהֹוָ֨ה צְבָא֜וֹת לְכׇל־הָֽעַמִּים֙ בָּהָ֣ר הַזֶּ֔ה מִשְׁתֵּ֥ה שְׁמָנִ֖ים מִשְׁתֵּ֣ה שְׁמָרִ֑ים שְׁמָנִים֙ מְמֻ֣חָיִ֔ם שְׁמָרִ֖ים מְזֻקָּקִֽים׃
verses.king_james: And in this mountain shall the Lord of hosts make unto all people a feast of fat things, a feast of wines on the lees, of fat things full of marrow, of wines on the lees well refined.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et præcipitabit in monte isto  faciem vinculi colligati super omnes populos,  et telam quam orditus est super omnes nationes.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וּבִלַּע֙ בָּהָ֣ר הַזֶּ֔ה פְּנֵֽי־הַלּ֥וֹט ׀ הַלּ֖וֹט עַל־כׇּל־הָעַמִּ֑ים וְהַמַּסֵּכָ֥ה הַנְּסוּכָ֖ה עַל־כׇּל־הַגּוֹיִֽם׃
verses.king_james: And he will destroy in this mountain the face of the covering cast over all people, and the vail that is spread over all nations.

verses.latin_vulgate: Præcipitabit mortem in sempiternum ;  et auferet Dominus Deus  lacrimam ab omni facie,  et opprobrium populi sui auferet  de universa terra :  quia Dominus locutus est.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: בִּלַּ֤ע הַמָּ֙וֶת֙ לָנֶ֔צַח וּמָחָ֨ה אֲדֹנָ֧י יֱהֹוִ֛ה דִּמְעָ֖ה מֵעַ֣ל כׇּל־פָּנִ֑ים וְחֶרְפַּ֣ת עַמּ֗וֹ יָסִיר֙ מֵעַ֣ל כׇּל־הָאָ֔רֶץ כִּ֥י יְהֹוָ֖ה דִּבֵּֽר׃ {פ}
verses.king_james: He will swallow up death in victory; and the Lord God will wipe away tears from off all faces; and the rebuke of his people shall he take away from off all the earth: for the Lord hath spoken it.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et dicet in die illa :  Ecce Deus noster iste ;  exspectavimus eum, et salvabit nos ;  iste Dominus, sustinuimus eum :  exsultabimus, et lætabimur in salutari ejus.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְאָמַר֙ בַּיּ֣וֹם הַה֔וּא הִנֵּ֨ה אֱלֹהֵ֥ינוּ זֶ֛ה קִוִּ֥ינוּ ל֖וֹ וְיוֹשִׁיעֵ֑נוּ זֶ֤ה יְהֹוָה֙ קִוִּ֣ינוּ ל֔וֹ נָגִ֥ילָה וְנִשְׂמְחָ֖ה בִּישׁוּעָתֽוֹ׃
verses.king_james: And it shall be said in that day, Lo, this is our God; we have waited for him, and he will save us: this is the Lord; we have waited for him, we will be glad and rejoice in his salvation.

verses.latin_vulgate: Quia requiescet manus Domini  in monte isto ;  et triturabitur Moab sub eo,  sicuti teruntur paleæ in plaustro.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כִּי־תָנ֥וּחַ יַד־יְהֹוָ֖ה בָּהָ֣ר הַזֶּ֑ה וְנָד֤וֹשׁ מוֹאָב֙ תַּחְתָּ֔יו כְּהִדּ֥וּשׁ מַתְבֵּ֖ן (במי) [בְּמ֥וֹ] מַדְמֵנָֽה׃
verses.king_james: For in this mountain shall the hand of the Lord rest, and Moab shall be trodden down under him, even as straw is trodden down for the dunghill.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et extendet manus suas sub eo  sicut extendit natans ad natandum ;  et humiliabit gloriam ejus  cum allisione manuum ejus.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וּפֵרַ֤שׂ יָדָיו֙ בְּקִרְבּ֔וֹ כַּאֲשֶׁ֛ר יְפָרֵ֥שׂ הַשֹּׂחֶ֖ה לִשְׂח֑וֹת וְהִשְׁפִּיל֙ גַּאֲוָת֔וֹ עִ֖ם אׇרְבּ֥וֹת יָדָֽיו׃
verses.king_james: And he shall spread forth his hands in the midst of them, as he that swimmeth spreadeth forth his hands to swim: and he shall bring down their pride together with the spoils of their hands.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et munimenta sublimium murorum tuorum  concident, et humiliabuntur,  et detrahentur in terram usque ad pulverem.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וּמִבְצַ֞ר מִשְׂגַּ֣ב חוֹמֹתֶ֗יךָ הֵשַׁ֥ח הִשְׁפִּ֛יל הִגִּ֥יעַ לָאָ֖רֶץ עַד־עָפָֽר׃ {ס}
verses.king_james: And the fortress of the high fort of thy walls shall he bring down, lay low, and bring to the ground, even to the dust.
